statement.markdown: Given a queue of integers, implement a method to dequeue an element from the front of the queue. The method should return the removed element.
statement.inputFormat: A queue of integers, represented as a character array.
statement.outputFormat: The removed integer element, represented as a string.
statement.notes: The queue is null-terminated, meaning that it ends with a special character '0'.

examples.explanation: Dequeue an element from the front of the queue.

hints.text: Think about how to represent a queue and how to implement the dequeue operation.
Consider the edge case when the queue is empty.

editorial.title: Editorial: Faulty FIFO Dequeue Implementation
editorial.intuition: A queue is a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) data structure. To dequeue an element, we need to remove it from the front of the queue.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to shift all elements in the queue to the left after removing the front element.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a deque to implement the queue, which provides an efficient way to remove elements from the front.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is based on the properties of a queue and the deque data structure.
